key_findings.summary: JP OCR error '幅輳' should be '輻輳' (network congestion). ZH/EN translations self-corrected to correct meaning ('网络拥塞' / 'Congestion'), so this is a JP-side surface defect, not a translation defect. Release-impacting because JP surface is the canonical lookup key.

EN translations on IPv6/IPv4/IPアドレスの枯渇問題 use Title-Case common nouns ('IP Address', 'Internet Protocol', 'Decimal') mid-prose, plus one ungrammatical phrase 'expressed in Decimal number'. Glossary-style capitalization bleeding into body text. Faithfulness fine; fluency lacking.

observations: ZH translation quality is uniformly strong across all 8 pages — no zh polish findings.

EN translation quality is mostly strong but shows recurring Title-Case-common-noun pattern on technical-term pages (p492). Suggest pipeline-level prompt note: 'in definition body, common nouns referring to defined terms should be lowercase unless they are proper nouns or acronyms'.
Acronym handling is good (HTML, MTBF, LAN/WAN, FHD, VGA family all preserved with optional zh expansion).
Formula preservation is precise (MTBF, transmission efficiency, transmission time).
No choice markers (ア/イ/ウ/エ) appeared on any of the 8 assigned pages, so the choice-marker normalization rule could not be tested in this sample.
kana_helper was null on every term across all 8 pages — no kana_helper.zh_concept claims to verify.
